case_name: Professional Conduct Committee appointed by the Dental Council of New Zealand v Subramani [2022] NZHPDT 1282 (16 December 2022)

summary.brief: Professional Conduct Committee appointed by the Dental Council of New Zealand v Subramani [2022] NZHPDT 1282 (16 December 2022). Medical malpractice case involving oncology.

evidence_index.text: The clinical records do not record the requirement information contained in the NZDC guidelines on sedation which are: (i) Initial assessment findings (ii) The patient’s written informed consent
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: There is a note about a root filling, but no tooth nomenclature, but it is assumed it is tooth 43. These notes are exactly the same notes as another patient ([G] discussed below).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: There was a preoperative x-ray of tooth 26 but Dr Subramani had not recorded any detail in his notes as to why this was taken, or any findings of the x-ray;
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: If he had told her she had an option as to when she had the treatment, she would not have had it done before she saw the periodontist.
evidence_index.speaker: Patient Ms [J]

evidence_index.text: The practitioner has been guilty of professional misconduct because of any act or omission that, in the judgment of the Tribunal, amounts to malpractice or negligence in relation to the scope of practice.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal

evidence_index.text: The Tribunal must assess whether the departure from acceptable standards has been significant enough to warrant a finding of professional misconduct against the practitioner.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal

evidence_index.text: Dr Subramani significantly breached the trust of his patients who sought dental treatment for an array of reasons, some requiring urgent treatment following an accident.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The conduct alleged above either separately or cumulatively amounts to professional misconduct pursuant to section 100(1)(a) and/or section 100(1)(b) of the Act.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented for dental treatment including root canal and fillings.

focused_exam.dental: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

reveal.detailed_findings: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient requires dental treatment including fillings
decision_points.prompt: What should be documented in the patient's dental records?

options.description: Document detailed treatment plan and obtain informed consent
options.clinical_reasoning: Ensures patient is informed and consents to treatment

options.description: Proceed with treatment without detailed documentation
options.clinical_reasoning: Lack of documentation can lead to misunderstandings and legal issues

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not adequately document treatment plan or obtain informed consent
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant failed to meet documentation standards

expected_action_court.description: Detailed documentation and informed consent should have been obtained

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to document adequately is a breach of professional standards
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Detailed records and informed consent should have been obtained
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Professional misconduct due to inadequate documentation

patient_outcome.description: Patient received inadequate dental treatment documentation
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Potential for future dental complications due to inadequate documentation

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to document treatment adequately

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to document dental treatment adequately
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper documentation would have ensured informed consent and appropriate treatment

treatments.treatment: Dental procedures including root canal and fillings

treatments.outcome: Inadequate documentation led to legal issues

complications.complication: Need for extensive remedial dental work

allegations.allegation: Failure to document treatment and obtain informed consent

court_findings.finding: Defendant breached duty of care by not documenting informed consent
court_findings.legal_significance: Informed Consent Practice Standard not satisfied

court_findings.finding: Professional misconduct due to inadequate documentation
Professional misconduct due to inadequate documentation
